title: AI value is stalling but the issue isn’t the technology
excerpt: Unlocking AI's potential through culture change.
content_html:
The AI fever of the last few years has stalled slightly, despite companies investing mind-boggling sums in tools and infrastructure.

But the problem is not with technology itself. Many companies are focused on experimentation and expansion, with only 9% of UK organizations reaching augmentation stage, according to ServiceNow’s most recent AI Maturity Index research.

The pattern is all too often the same: after an initial wave of experimentation, companies find that early enthusiasm fails to translate into sustained usage.

Chief Digital & Technology Officer UK&I, ServiceNow.

For example, employees stop using AI assistants, perhaps out of fear of ‘augmenting themselves out of work’.

It’s clear that the problem here is not that AI systems do not work: it’s that the cultural foundations needed to support them are lacking. The problem of finding real value from AI is often a cultural one.

This requires careful thought to build creativity back into people’s work lives, so they can re-engineer legacy processes to take control and truly reap the benefits of AI.

By doing so, they set the stage for a whole new way of working, and a new way to grow businesses.

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

Losing control

A key issue is that when employees feel like they are losing control over how they work, they tend to disengage. If employees feel like they are training their replacements, or losing any sense of agency they once had, they tend to push back or revert to older processes.

This is seen most clearly in organizations that have invested heavily in AI platforms: teams try the tools, then revert to familiar processes, manual workarounds, and even back to spreadsheets. This is a cultural problem.

People’s concerns over job erosion and loss of autonomy are real, and dealing with this requires trust, new skills, and redesigned workflows. Teams must be empowered to shift to new growth-focused ways of working.

Employees need to engage creative muscles in a way they may never have before, one which previous workflows and employment structures often barred from them.

Shifting mindset

This mindset shift needs to start at the top. All too often, CEOs still see AI as a ‘magic pixie dust’ which can be used for one reason: to cut costs. They are marooned in a post-COVID protectionist mindset, and the only thing that matters is shrinking the cost base.

What needs to happen is a shift to a growth mindset, using the economies of AI to find new approaches to the market. Rather than cutting costs or getting rid of people, organizations need to reshape how teams work. Bosses should identify new sets of use cases and go after bigger targets.

Rather than allowing people to make themselves redundant, they should retrain and invest in those people, pivoting that human capital to something different and more valuable. Your people know your business: you should invest in them.

That retraining cannot be treated as a one-off exercise. Organizations need to create structured pathways for continuous learning and AI skill development, helping employees build confidence and adapt alongside technology. Employees need personalized learning and hands-on experience that helps them move from experimentation to real-world application.

A new mindset

What is required to succeed with AI is a radically different mindset, focused on growth and the customer. This change needs to come from the board level, from the Chief Information Officer (CIO), Chief Digital Officer (CDO), or Chief Technology Officer (CTO).

Organizations need to move employees from doing one specific task to a new focus on growth and move beyond simple digital transformation to something bigger.

Culture and change management, rather than technology alone, will be what dictates the winners of the next phase of AI.

Leaders who have the foresight and vision to upskill their employees and drive towards new ways of working will see huge productivity gains and will position their companies for future success.

Culture, not technology

The solution to stalling AI, or a failure to find value from the technology is not more AI, bigger models and more pilots. It is a cultural solution, based on the best asset you already have: your people.

That means superpowering existing teams, not replacing them, and finding new business models. This requires upskilling staff, rebuilding workflows and engaged leadership that actively encourages new ways of working.

To build companies fit for the future, culture and change management are just as important as AI licenses.

We list the best HR software.

This article was produced as part of TechRadar Pro Perspectives, our channel to feature the best and brightest minds in the technology industry today.

The views expressed here are those of the author and are not necessarily those of TechRadarPro or Future plc. If you are interested in contributing find out more here: https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit

Chief Digital & Technology Officer UK&I, ServiceNow.

sentences.en: Bosses should identify new sets of use cases and go after bigger targets.
sentences.zh: 老板应该确定新的用例集并追求更大的目标。

sentences.en: Your people know your business: you should invest in them.
sentences.zh: 您的员工了解您的业务：您应该对他们进行投资。

Employees need personalized learning and hands-on experience that helps them move from experimentation to real-world application.

sentences.en: Employees need personalized learning and hands-on experience that helps them move from experimentation to real-world application.
sentences.zh: 员工需要个性化的学习和实践经验，帮助他们从实验转向现实应用。

blocks.html_en: Chief Digital & Technology Officer UK&I, ServiceNow.

sentences.en: Chief Digital & Technology Officer UK&I, ServiceNow.
sentences.zh: ServiceNow UK&I 首席数字与技术官。

content_html_zh:
尽管公司在工具和基础设施上投入了令人难以置信的资金，但过去几年的人工智能热潮略有停滞。

但问题不在于技术本身。 根据 ServiceNow 最新的人工智能成熟度指数研究，许多公司都专注于实验和扩张，只有 9% 的英国组织达到了增强阶段。

这种模式常常是相同的：经过最初的一波实验后，公司发现早期的热情未能转化为持续的使用。

ServiceNow UK&I 首席数字与技术官。

例如，员工停止使用人工智能助手，可能是因为担心“增强自己的工作能力”。

显然，这里的问题不是人工智能系统不起作用：而是缺乏支持它们所需的文化基础。 从人工智能中寻找真正价值的问题通常是一个文化问题。

这需要仔细思考，将创造力重新融入人们的工作生活中，以便他们能够重新设计遗留流程以掌控并真正获得人工智能的好处。

通过这样做，他们为全新的工作方式和业务发展的新方式奠定了基础。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

失去控制

一个关键问题是，当员工感觉自己失去了对工作方式的控制时，他们往往会脱离。 如果员工觉得自己正在培训继任者，或者失去了曾经拥有的任何代理意识，他们往往会推迟或恢复到旧的流程。

这在大量投资人工智能平台的组织中最为明显：团队尝试这些工具，然后恢复到熟悉的流程、手动解决方法，甚至回到电子表格。 这是一个文化问题。

人们对工作侵蚀和自主权丧失的担忧是真实存在的，而解决这个问题需要信任、新技能和重新设计的工作流程。 必须授权团队转向以增长为中心的新工作方式。

员工需要以一种他们以前从未有过的方式发挥创造力，而以前的工作流程和雇佣结构常常禁止他们这样做。

转变心态

这种思维方式的转变需要从高层开始。 很多时候，首席执行官们仍然将人工智能视为“神奇的仙尘”，使用它的原因只有一个：削减成本。 他们陷入了后疫情时代的保护主义心态，唯一重要的是缩小成本基础。

需要发生的是转变为增长思维，利用人工智能经济来寻找进入市场的新方法。 组织需要重塑团队的工作方式，而不是削减成本或裁员。 老板应该确定新的用例集并追求更大的目标。

他们不应该让人们自我冗余，而应该对这些人进行再培训和投资，将人力资本转向不同的、更有价值的领域。 您的员工了解您的业务：您应该对他们进行投资。

这种再培训不能被视为一次性的练习。 组织需要为持续学习和人工智能技能发展创建结构化途径，帮助员工建立信心并适应技术。 员工需要个性化的学习和实践经验，帮助他们从实验转向现实应用。

新的心态

人工智能要想取得成功，需要一种完全不同的思维方式，专注于增长和客户。 这种变革需要来自董事会层面，来自首席信息官 (CIO)、首席数字官 (CDO) 或首席技术官 (CTO)。

组织需要让员工从执行一项特定任务转向关注增长，并超越简单的数字化转型，转向更大的目标。

文化和变革管理，而不仅仅是技术，将决定人工智能下一阶段的赢家。

具有远见和远见、能够提高员工技能并推动新工作方式的领导者将看到生产力的巨大提升，并将为公司奠定未来成功的基础。

文化，而非技术

阻碍人工智能发展或无法从技术中发现价值的解决方案不是更多的人工智能、更大的模型和更多的飞行员。 这是一种文化解决方案，基于您已有的最佳资产：您的员工。

这意味着增强现有团队的能力，而不是取代他们，并寻找新的业务模式。 这需要提高员工技能、重建工作流程和积极鼓励新工作方式的积极领导。

为了打造适应未来的公司，文化和变革管理与人工智能许可证同样重要。
